Also: pin how these anchors and styles interact with your existing NodeIdAllocator (the port ids come from the same id space — verify against captures).

Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

280	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
281	pub enum GraphNode {
282	    /// A rule instance built from [`Fact`]s (rendered + wrapped by this crate).
283	    Rule(RuleInstance),
284	    /// A rule instance whose cells are pre-rendered strings (still wrapped/escaped).
285	    RawRule(RawRule),
286	    /// `!KU( term ) @ #t` — intruder knowledge (drawn as a gray ellipse). `term`
287	    /// is the pre-rendered message text.
288	    Knowledge { term: String, temporal: String },
289	    /// `Fact @ #t` — a protocol action/event (darkblue ellipse). `fact` is
290	    /// pre-rendered.
291	    Action { fact: String, temporal: String },
292	    /// `#t : rule` — a compressed rule shown as a single uncolored ellipse.
293	    Compressed { temporal: String, rule: String },
294	    /// `#var` — a bare timepoint ellipse (uncolored). Observed for ordinary
295	    /// timepoint variables (`#i`, `#decrypt`, `#t1`, …) and, when a constraint
296	    /// system carries a designated last timepoint (induction), the `#last` node
297	    /// (`#last` is the target of `color="black",style="dashed"` before-edges).
298	    Temporal { var: String },
299	    /// `(#var, idx)` — an unresolved node referenced by an open premise
300	    /// (invtrapezium), the target of a conclusion→absent-node structural edge.
301	    OpenTarget { node_var: String, premise_index: usize },
302	    /// A node with an explicit `shape` and label — the extension point for shapes
303	    /// beyond the observed set (e.g. the `trapezium` dual, an unresolved source
304	    /// feeding a present premise, which is spec-named but was **not observed** in
305	    /// the corpus or any probe; see BEHAVIOR.md §3d).
306	    Shaped { label: String, shape: String, color: Option<String> },
307	}
308	
309	impl GraphNode {
310	    /// The designated last timepoint `#last` (BEHAVIOR.md §3d/§6).
311	    pub fn last() -> Self {
312	        GraphNode::Temporal { var: "last".into() }
313	    }
314	    /// A record node from a [`RecordSpec`], if this node is one.
315	    fn record_spec(&self) -> Option<RecordSpec> {
316	        match self {
317	            GraphNode::Rule(r) => Some(r.spec()),
318	            GraphNode::RawRule(r) => Some(r.spec()),
319	            _ => None,
320	        }
321	    }
322	}
323	
324	/// An endpoint reference into the input system (resolved to `n<K>[:n<port>]`).
325	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
326	pub enum EndRef {
327	    /// The whole node (no port) — ellipses, invtrapezium, legend.
328	    Whole(usize),
329	    /// Conclusion `c` of rule node `n` (→ its conclusion port).

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-13 census: classify every ported edge endpoint by port-class.

For each .dot file:
  1. Parse every record node -> map port_id -> class in {prem, info, concl}.
  2. For each edge `A[:pa] -> B[:pb][attrs]`, classify src/dst port (if any)
     as prem/info/concl (of the record it belongs to) or 'unknownport', and
     record the attr-set (style).
Emit corpus-wide counts keyed by (position, style, portclass).
"""
import os, re, sys, collections

CORPUS = "/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/oracle/dot_corpus"

# record node line: nID[shape="record",label="...",...]
rec_re = re.compile(r'^(n\d+)\[shape="record",label="(.*)",fillcolor=', re.S)
# edge line: SRC -> DST[attrs];   where SRC/DST = nID or nID:nPORT
edge_re = re.compile(r'^(n\d+)(?::(n\d+))? -> (n\d+)(?::(n\d+))?(\[[^\]]*\])?;$')

def parse_record_ports(label):
    """Return dict port_id -> class. label is the raw record label text
    between the outer quotes (record syntax, with \\| \\{ \\} \\< \\> escapes)."""
    # Walk chars, tracking brace depth over UNescaped braces; collect columns
    # at depth 1 (top-level groups) split by depth-1 '|'. Then within each
    # column, the ports at depth-2 cells. We only need per-column port lists.
    # Simplify: find all '<nX>' tokens with their enclosing top-level column idx.
    depth = 0
    col_idx = -1
    ports_by_col = collections.defaultdict(list)
    i = 0
    n = len(label)
    while i < n:
        c = label[i]
        if c == '\\':
            i += 2
            continue
        if c == '{':
            depth += 1
            if depth == 1:
                col_idx = 0
            i += 1
            continue
        if c == '}':
            depth -= 1
            i += 1
            continue
        if c == '|' and depth == 1:
            col_idx += 1
            i += 1
            continue
        if c == '<':
            # port token <nX>
            j = label.find('>', i)
            tok = label[i+1:j]
            if re.fullmatch(r'n\d+', tok):
                ports_by_col[col_idx].append(tok)
            i = j + 1
            continue
        i += 1
    # Classify: with C columns, col0=prem, mid=info, last=concl IF 3 cols.
    # Observed groups: build_record keeps info always; prem/concl dropped if
    # empty. So #columns in {1,2,3}. The INFO column is the one whose single
    # cell's text matches "#... : ..." — but structurally we map by position:
    #   3 cols -> [prem, info, concl]
    #   2 cols -> ambiguous; disambiguate below by content
    #   1 col  -> info only
    cols = sorted(ports_by_col.keys())
    result = {}
    ncol = len(cols)
    # Grab the flat text of each column's first cell to detect info ("#x : ...").
    return ports_by_col, ncol

def col_texts(label):
    """Return list of (col_idx -> list of cell raw-texts)."""
    depth = 0
    col_idx = -1
    texts = collections.defaultdict(list)
    cur = []
    i = 0; n = len(label)
    buf = ''
    # simpler: reuse port walk but also capture text after each <port>
    # We'll capture full column raw substring instead.
    # Redo: split top-level into columns.
    columns = []
    start = None
    i = 0
    while i < n:
        c = label[i]
        if c == '\\':
            i += 2; continue
        if c == '{':
            depth += 1
            if depth == 1:
                columns.append('')
                start = i+1
            i += 1; continue
        if c == '}':
            if depth == 1 and start is not None:
                columns[-1] = label[start:i]
                start = None
            depth -= 1
            i += 1; continue
        if c == '|' and depth == 1:
            # new column starts after this at next '{'
            i += 1; continue
        i += 1
    return columns

INFO_CELL = re.compile(r'<n\d+>\s*#')

def classify(label):
    """port_id -> class. Uses column structure + info detection."""
    ports_by_col, ncol = parse_record_ports(label)
    columns = col_texts(label)
    result = {}
    # Determine which column is 'info': the one whose cell text begins with '#'
    info_col = None
    for ci, ctext in enumerate(columns):
        # first cell text after its <port>
        m = re.match(r'\s*<n\d+>\s*(.*)', ctext, re.S)
        # info column cell always looks like "#tvar : rulename[...]"
        # but a concl/prem cell "State_(..)" or "Out(..)" etc. Info starts '#'.
        if m and m.group(1).lstrip().startswith('#'):
            # could match a fact containing # ... but facts don't start with #
            info_col = ci
            break
    for ci in sorted(ports_by_col.keys()):
        for p in ports_by_col[ci]:
            if ci == info_col:
                result[p] = 'info'
            elif info_col is not None and ci < info_col:
                result[p] = 'prem'
            elif info_col is not None and ci > info_col:
                result[p] = 'concl'
            else:
                result[p] = 'unk'
    return result, info_col, ncol

pos_style_class = collections.Counter()
files_with_info_src = 0
total_files = 0
info_src_edges = 0
info_dst_edges = 0
# track: does info port ever appear as target?
info_as_target_examples = []
info_as_source_styles = collections.Counter()

files = sorted(os.listdir(CORPUS))
for fn in files:
    if not fn.endswith('.dot'):
        continue
    total_files += 1
    path = os.path.join(CORPUS, fn)
    with open(path, encoding='utf-8') as f:
        lines = f.read().splitlines()
    portclass = {}   # port_id -> class
    for ln in lines:
        m = rec_re.match(ln)
        if m:
            cls, info_col, ncol = classify(m.group(2))
            portclass.update(cls)
    this_file_info_src = False
    for ln in lines:
        m = edge_re.match(ln.strip())
        if not m:
            continue
        srcn, srcp, dstn, dstp, attrs = m.groups()
        attrs = attrs or ''
        if srcp:
            cl = portclass.get(srcp, 'noRecPort')
            pos_style_class[('src', attrs, cl)] += 1
            if cl == 'info':
                info_src_edges += 1
                this_file_info_src = True
                info_as_source_styles[attrs] += 1
        if dstp:
            cl = portclass.get(dstp, 'noRecPort')
            pos_style_class[('dst', attrs, cl)] += 1
            if cl == 'info':
                info_dst_edges += 1
                if len(info_as_target_examples) < 10:
                    info_as_target_examples.append((fn, ln.strip()))
    if this_file_info_src:
        files_with_info_src += 1

print("TOTAL FILES:", total_files)
print("FILES WITH >=1 info-source edge:", files_with_info_src,
      f"({100.0*files_with_info_src/total_files:.1f}%)")
print("INFO-SOURCE edges total:", info_src_edges)
print("INFO-TARGET edges total:", info_dst_edges)
print()
print("=== info-as-SOURCE by style ===")
for a, c in info_as_source_styles.most_common():
    print(f"{c:8d}  {a}")
print()
print("=== info-as-TARGET examples ===")
for fn, ln in info_as_target_examples:
    print(fn, ln)
print()
print("=== FULL (position, style, portclass) census ===")
for (pos, style, cl), c in sorted(pos_style_class.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    print(f"{c:8d}  {pos:3s} {cl:10s} {style}")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-13 census: classify every ported edge endpoint by port-class.

Info cell is the one whose text starts with '#' (a temporal var `#t : rule`);
prem/concl by column position relative to the info column.
"""
import os, re, collections

CORPUS = "/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/oracle/dot_corpus"

rec_re = re.compile(r'^(n\d+)\[shape="record",label="(.*)",fillcolor=', re.S)
edge_re = re.compile(r'^(n\d+)(?::(n\d+))? -> (n\d+)(?::(n\d+))?(\[[^\]]*\])?;$')

def parse_record(label):
    """Return dict port_id -> class ('prem'|'info'|'concl').
    Walk top-level columns (depth-1 '|' splits); a port's cell text is the
    chars after '<nX>'; info column = the one whose first cell starts '#'."""
    depth = 0
    col_idx = 0
    # (col_idx, port, first_text_char)
    ports = []
    i, n = 0, len(label)
    while i < n:
        c = label[i]
        if c == '\\':
            i += 2; continue
        if c == '{':
            depth += 1; i += 1; continue
        if c == '}':
            depth -= 1; i += 1; continue
        if c == '|' and depth == 1:
            col_idx += 1; i += 1; continue
        if c == '<':
            j = label.find('>', i)
            tok = label[i+1:j]
            k = j + 1
            while k < n and label[k] == ' ':
                k += 1
            first = label[k] if k < n else ''
            if re.fullmatch(r'n\d+', tok):
                ports.append((col_idx, tok, first))
            i = j + 1; continue
        i += 1
    # info column = min col_idx whose a cell first char == '#'
    info_col = None
    for col_idx, tok, first in ports:
        if first == '#':
            info_col = col_idx
            break
    out = {}
    for col_idx, tok, first in ports:
        if info_col is None:
            out[tok] = 'unk'
        elif col_idx == info_col:
            out[tok] = 'info'
        elif col_idx < info_col:
            out[tok] = 'prem'
        else:
            out[tok] = 'concl'
    return out

pos_style_class = collections.Counter()
files_with_info_src = 0
total_files = 0
info_src_edges = 0
info_dst_edges = 0
info_as_target_examples = []
info_as_source_styles = collections.Counter()
# also: for info-source edges, what is the target port class?
info_src_target = collections.Counter()

for fn in sorted(os.listdir(CORPUS)):
    if not fn.endswith('.dot'):
        continue
    total_files += 1
    with open(os.path.join(CORPUS, fn), encoding='utf-8') as f:
        lines = f.read().splitlines()
    portclass = {}
    for ln in lines:
        m = rec_re.match(ln)
        if m:
            portclass.update(parse_record(m.group(2)))
    this_info_src = False
    for ln in lines:
        m = edge_re.match(ln.strip())
        if not m:
            continue
        srcn, srcp, dstn, dstp, attrs = m.groups()
        attrs = attrs or '(none)'
        scl = portclass.get(srcp, 'noRecPort') if srcp else 'NOPORT'

### 3h. Edge-style completion + record info-port anchor (Session 13) — byte-verified over 12 022
Two capture-observable model gaps, both surfaced only by whole-graph
regeneration (the opaque round-trip preserves parsed edges verbatim and so
masks them). Primary source: the `oracle/dot_corpus/` census (captured OUTPUT).

**(1) Three previously-dropped styles.** The 8-of-11 vocabulary was completed
with `purple`/`dashed` (2416), `dotted`/`green` (2394, the reversed-key set),
and `darkorange3`/`dashed` (1553). `green`/`dotted` and `darkorange3`/`dashed`
are observed ONLY between plain ellipse nodes (never ported); `purple`/`dashed`
behaves like the other dashed temporal edges (info↔info, below). Witness
`01c5db0a7030e664` carries the dropped `n12 -> n33[color="darkorange3",style="dashed"]`.

**(2) The record info-port anchor.** An edge endpoint can anchor at a record's
interior **info cell** (the middle single-cell group holding `#tvar : rule`),
e.g. `n131:n128 -> n4` — witness `00082e1d6a47b5af` (record `n131`'s info cell
is `<n128> #vr.2 : …`). Port-class census of every ported endpoint (classifying
each port as prem / info / concl of its record):

- **94.2 %** of graphs (11 322 / 12 022) contain ≥ 1 info-source edge;
  58 206 info-source edges, 18 888 info-target edges corpus-wide.
- The info port is used at **both** ends, not source-only. Info-**source** by
  style: `blue3`/`dashed` 36694, `invis` 13354 (legend), `black`/`dashed` 5752,
  `purple`/`dashed` 2405, `red`/`dashed` 1 (a lone legitimate case, record `n10`
  in `1d4a080feaef2b99`). Info-**target** by style: `blue3`/`dashed` 11525,
  `black`/`dashed` 4950, `purple`/`dashed` 2413. So the dashed temporal edges
  (`blue3`/`black`/`purple`) anchor at the info port whenever the endpoint is a
  rule record, and at the whole node (NOPORT) when it is an ellipse timepoint;
  the legend `invis` edge anchors its **source** at the info port when it starts
  from a rule record.
- Structural bold edges are unchanged: **source = a conclusion port, target = a
  premise port** (never info). Message/`gray30`: source ellipse (NOPORT) or a
  conclusion port, target a premise port or ellipse. `orangered2`: conclusion
  port or ellipse → ellipse.
- **Port-id relation (same NodeIdAllocator id space, §3e):** for a record with
  node id `N`, `P` premise cells, one info cell, `C` conclusion cells, the info
  port is `N − C − 1` (ports are `N−(P+1+C) … N−1`, allocated prem, info, concl;
  empty prem/concl groups are dropped but the info group is always present).
  Verified: `n131` (C=2 → 131−3=128), `n148` (C=1 → 146), `n117` (C=1 → 115).
